decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, alongside powerful spells like Thraben Charm and Galvanic Discharge. The deck aims to maintain board control with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ranger-Captain of Eos, while also utilizing Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride for additional support and damage output. The inclusion of cards like Haliya, Guided by Light and The Legend of Roku adds versatility and unpredictability to the deck's strategy, making it a formidable force in Modern 1 vs 1 gameplay.

This deck focuses on quickly reanimating high value creatures using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides additional synergy with +1/+1 counters to boost the reanimated creatures. Support cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation help to protect the combo pieces, while Solitude and Faithful Mending provide additional ways to control the board and gain card advantage. The deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful board state and overwhelm the opponent before they can respond effectively.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction while maintaining board control with creatures like Solitude. The deck also utilizes its companion, Kaheera, the Orphanguard, to provide additional value and synergy with its creature-heavy strategy. Overall, the deck aims to control the battlefield, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and eventually win the game through attrition and resource denial.

This deck revolves around quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. The deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize while setting up its combo. Cards like Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide card advantage, while Solitude offers removal. The deck also includes a variety of lands to ensure mana consistency. Overall, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively play artifacts, taking advantage of affinity mechanics to flood the board with threats. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of artifacts, while cards like Mishra's Bauble and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide card advantage and selection. The inclusion of disruptive elements like Metallic Rebuke and Unholy Heat helps control the board while applying pressure. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of speed, card advantage, and disruption to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing efficient ritual spells and mana ramping effects, along with card draw and deck manipulation, to quickly generate mana and storm count to cast a lethal Grapeshot. Cards like Manamorphose and Pyretic Ritual help fuel the storm count, while Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion provide additional value and mana reduction. Past in Flames allows for graveyard recursion, while Artist's Talent can copy key spells for explosive turns. The deck aims to combo off quickly and overwhelm the opponent with a high storm count, ultimately leading to a decisive victory.

The main concept of this UR aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while utilizing efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to clear the way or finish off the opponent. The deck also includes card selection and cantrips like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to ensure consistency in drawing threats and answers. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain it throughout the game, utilizing a mix of creatures and burn spells to close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage using Teferi, Time Raveler and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. It also utilizes Isochron Scepter with powerful instants like Orim's Chant for additional control. The deck includes a variety of lands to ensure consistent mana base and options for fetching lands. The inclusion of a companion, Kaheera, the Orphanguard, adds an additional creature to the deck that synergizes well with the control elements. Ultimately, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through efficient control and resource management.

This deck focuses on aggressive strategies while also utilizing blink effects to gain value from entering the battlefield abilities. The deck includes a mix of creatures with powerful abilities and cards that can blink those creatures, allowing them to re-use their abilities for additional value. The mana base is designed to support both white and blue mana requirements, with fetch lands to ensure consistency. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent while maintaining flexibility and resilience through blink effects.

The deck focuses on a combination of aggressive creatures and cards that allow players to protect and reuse those creatures effectively. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Springleaf Drum, the deck can repeatedly trigger enter-the-battlefield effects and generate tempo advantages. Starfield Shepherd and Guide of Souls provide additional protection for key creatures, while cards like Solitude and Consign to Memory offer removal options when needed. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mixture of aggressive creatures and strategic blinking tactics to keep threats on the board and disrupt opponents' game plans.

The deck focuses on utilizing Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate a powerful combo with Blade of the Bloodchief, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming board presence. By using cards like Urza's Saga and Eldrazi Temple to ramp into big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with its combo potential. Additionally, cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble provide disruption and control elements to the deck, allowing it to both protect its combo pieces and hinder the opponent's game plan.

The main idea behind this deck is to combine aggressive creatures with cards that can manipulate the board state through blinking effects, such as Ephemerate and Flickerwisp. This allows the player to control the battlefield and disrupt their opponent's plans while maintaining pressure with their creatures. The deck also includes a mix of removal, card draw, and protection spells to support this strategy. Additionally, the inclusion of powerful creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd enhances the deck's ability to close out games quickly.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures with blink effects to repeatedly remove threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining creatures like Emperor of Bones and Overlord of the Balemurk with cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp, the deck aims to control the board while dealing damage to the opponent. The inclusion of Thoughtseize and Fatal Push adds additional disruption and removal options, while cards like Quantum Riddler and Witch Enchanter provide card advantage and utility. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm and outmaneuver opponents through aggressive tactics and strategic blinking.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and synergies to create a fast and aggressive playstyle. By using cards like Arcbound Ravager and Emry, Lurker of the Loch, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and overwhelm the opponent. Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke provide control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble help generate card advantage and fuel the deck's strategy. With a focus on efficiently utilizing artifacts and creating a powerful board state, this deck aims to quickly close out games.

The main concept of this deck is to ramp up quickly using the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) and Expedition Map to quickly play powerful colorless threats like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also uses disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer to control the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes some removal and utility cards like Dismember and Warping Wail to deal with threats. The key strategy is to lock down the opponent's board while establishing a strong presence with powerful threats.

The deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate massive amounts of colorless mana, allowing for powerful plays with Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. The deck utilizes control elements such as Chalice of the Void and Karn, the Great Creator to disrupt opponents while also providing win conditions with Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. The deck's versatile removal options and high impact threats make it a formidable choice for dominating the late game and overwhelming opponents.
